author: juzigu40-ui
title: Anti-gaming attestation is syntactic-only and auto-generated by --confirm
notes: All 4 code references verified: attestation checks only 2 substrings ('i have actually', 'not gaming'), --confirm auto-generates a passing attestation from --note, resolve_issues() mutates state with this attestation, and score guide treats strict/verified as trust tiers. However, manual resolutions are explicitly marked scan_verified=False, so the 'verified' score mode already mitigates this. The attestation is an intentional UX speed bump, not a security boundary. --confirm still requires --note (50+ chars). Overstated as 'structural trust-boundary failure' when verified score provides the real integrity measure.
